danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threats requiring avoidance or weapon use

danger_signals.visual_form: Variety of guns with visible ammo counters or depletion
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity creates vulnerability; player must choose when to engage

danger_signals.visual_form: Unstable stacked objects, toppling physics, slopes causing object scatter
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental instability poses danger; careless object manipulation creates fall/collision risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Inclined surfaces causing object toppling and player navigation difficulty
danger_signals.meaning: Traversal becomes hazardous; physics-based platforming demands precision

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items granting temporary invincibility or hit-point buffers
safety_signals.meaning: Increased survivability; momentary immunity to danger
Enhanced mobility for evasion and puzzle solving
Resource for continued play; carried out of level for score/life gain

safety_signals.visual_form: Chest objects containing contextual puzzle information
safety_signals.meaning: Information access reduces puzzle danger and dead-ends

Indigenous to 'The Planet'; thieves of Puggsy's spaceship (antagonistic relationship)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Puggsy character on 'The Planet' after crash/theft
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must solve puzzles and navigate platforming challenges to retrieve spaceship

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must locate, carry, and position objects to satisfy level completion conditions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Exit trigger activated when puzzle solved
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Score awarded; heart objects carried out grant extra lives; progression to next level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 16 secret levels accessed via passwords or hidden paths; special 'HEROS' block arrangement visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlocks alternate ending sequences; triggers cinematic rewards (cannot be terminated except by reset)

environmental_storytelling.example: Diverse physics-based objects (shells, weights, matches, keys, balloons, cups, barrels, knives, candles) establish a lived-in world with multiple functional purposes
Wabbits World level references game Wiz 'n' Liz, indicating environmental context and world connections
Slopes and stacking mechanics create natural environmental storytelling—objects topple naturally, communicating physical laws of the planet
Spaceship theft by raccoon natives establishes motivation; secret ending cinematics (cinematic sequences with mandatory termination) provide narrative payoff

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Platformer navigation with gravity physics and object carrying capacity
Movement speed/agility may change; slopes cause dropped/scattered inventory
Unspecified by source; standard platformer enemy patterns inferred (patrol, pursuit, or static hazard)
Toppling on slopes; scattering when improperly balanced; weight-dependent movement response
6 boss encounters (9 in Mega-CD version); optional for game completion; visual design unspecified

notes: Puggsy communicates world-state primarily through physics-based environmental interaction rather than explicit visual narrative. Danger is signaled through enemy presence and environmental instability (toppling objects, slopes). Safety derives from power-up collection and successful puzzle-solving. The raccoon theft plot establishes low-intensity political antagonism without faction visuals (species-based rather than symbolic). The game's color choice (orange for Puggsy) was deliberately chosen to prevent hardware color-bleeding on Genesis, indicating technical constraints shaped visual communication. Secret endings and cinematic rewards provide meta-narrative feedback for exploration and achievement. World-state progression is primarily puzzle-driven rather than environmental-storytelling driven.
